text: this is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: [0] 这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Neil. And I'm Beth. Neil, what is the oldest thing you have at home?
zh: [1] 我是尼尔。我是贝丝。尼尔,你家里最古老的东西是什么?

text: Ooh, well, I do have some books from when I was a child.
zh: [2] 哦,我确实有一些小时候的书。

text: I have a rocking horse that was my dad's, so that is quite old.
zh: [3] 我还有一匹摇摇马,是我爸爸的,所以那也算相当old了。

text: But neither of these are anywhere near as old as the 1800
zh: [4] 不过这两样东西都远远比不上那枚有1800年历史

text: year old Roman coin which BBC reporter Ryan Keane recently bought in London
zh: [5] 的罗马硬币古老,那是BBC记者瑞安·基恩最近在伦敦买到的。

text: but soon afterwards Ryan discovered there was more to the story as he explains here on BBC World Service programme Business Daily
zh: [6] 但不久之后,瑞安发现这背后还有更多故事,他在BBC世界服务频道的《商业日报》节目中做了讲述。

text: but as I researched into other kinds of ancient artifacts I could acquire
zh: [7] 在我调查还能获取哪些其他古代文物时

text: I came across reports of expansive criminal networks related to antiquities
zh: [8] 我发现了一些关于与古物相关的庞大犯罪网络的报道

text: Some ancient artefacts being sold online and in stores have been looted and trafficked.
zh: [9] 一些在网上和商店中出售的古代文物,其实是被盗掘和非法贩运而来的。

text: Ryan got interested in artefacts from Egypt, Greece and Rome.
zh: [10] 瑞安对来自埃及、希腊和罗马的文物产生了兴趣。

text: An artefact is any object made by humans, usually very old, which is historically interesting – for example, a coin or statue.
zh: [11] 文物指的是任何由人类制作、通常年代久远、具有历史意义的物品——比如硬币或雕像。

text: In the past, many artefacts have been looted or stolen by force.
zh: [12] 过去,许多文物都曾被劫掠或强行盗取。

text: Movies like Indiana Jones and Tomb Raider have made the subject popular,
zh: [13] 像《夺宝奇兵》和《古墓丽影》这样的电影让这个话题广为人知,

text: but the theft of ancient objects is a serious problem in some parts of the world.
zh: [14] 但在世界某些地区,古物盗窃仍是一个严重的问题。

text: So what can be done about it? That's what we'll be discussing in this episode using some useful new words and phrases.
zh: [15] 那么我们能做些什么呢?这就是本期节目要讨论的内容,我们会用到一些实用的新词汇和短语。

text: And remember you'll find all the vocabulary from this episode on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: [16] 别忘了,本期节目的所有词汇都可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到。

text: Now I have a question for you, Beth. One of the most famous ancient artefacts
zh: [17] 贝丝,我这里有个问题要考考你。最著名的古代文物之一

text: was discovered covered by Egyptologist Howard Carter in the Valley of the Kings in 1922. But what was it?
zh: [18] 是1922年埃及学家霍华德·卡特在帝王谷发现的。但那是什么呢?

text: Was it a the Rosetta Stone, b the Mask of Tutankhamun, or c the Ark of the Covenant?
zh: [19] 是A,罗塞塔石碑;B,图坦卡蒙的面具;还是C,约柜?

text: Oh, I think it's b the Mask of Tutankhamun.
zh: [20] 哦,我觉得是B,图坦卡蒙的面具。

text: OK, let's find out later in the programme. Stealing ancient objects is nothing new.
zh: [21] 好,我们稍后揭晓答案。盗取古代文物并不是什么新鲜事。

text: During colonial times, it was common for artefacts to be taken from their original home and moved to museums and private collections abroad.
zh: [22] 在殖民时期,文物被从原本所在地带走、运往海外的博物馆和私人收藏,是很常见的事。

text: In the modern era, technology, especially social media, has increased the problem.
zh: [23] 到了现代,科技,尤其是社交媒体,让这个问题变得更加严重。

text: Here's Amr al -Azam, a researcher from Shawnee State University in the US, explaining more to BBC's Business Daily.
zh: [24] 下面请听美国肖尼州立大学研究员阿姆尔·阿扎姆在BBC《商业日报》节目中的进一步解释。

text: Social media is the game -changer here.
zh: [25] 社交媒体在这里是一个改变游戏规则的因素。

text: The bottleneck, if you will, was how does someone who has an item then communicate with someone on the demand side?
zh: [26] 可以说,过去的瓶颈在于:一个拥有物品的人,怎么才能联系到有需求的一方?

text: It used to slow the process, you had to go through intermediaries.
zh: [27] 过去这个过程很慢,你必须通过中间人。

text: Social media changes all that because it just opens it
zh: [28] 社交媒体改变了这一切,因为它把渠道

text: up. Amr calls social media a game changer
zh: [29] 完全打开了。阿姆尔把社交媒体称为“game changer”(改变游戏规则的因素)

text: – something new that is introduced into a situation and completely changes how it works.
zh: [30] ——指的是引入某种情境后,彻底改变其运作方式的新事物。

text: For example, the internet has been a game changer in how we communicate.
zh: [31] 例如,互联网就彻底改变了我们沟通的方式。

text: The reason is simple. For someone with illegally stolen artefacts to sell, finding a buyer used to be difficult.
zh: [32] 原因很简单。对于那些手握非法盗取文物的人来说,过去很难找到买家。

text: Amma thinks that now, because of social media, this problem, or bottleneck, is gone.
zh: [33] 阿姆尔认为,现在由于社交媒体的存在,这个问题,也就是“瓶颈”,已经不复存在了。

text: A bottleneck is a problem that delays a process or stops it from progressing.
zh: [34] 瓶颈指的是延缓某个过程、或使其无法推进的问题。

text: So what can be done to stop the illegal buying and selling of ancient artefacts?
zh: [35] 那么,该如何阻止古代文物的非法买卖呢?

text: Well, as the final destination for much -looted treasure, museums have an important role to
zh: [36] 作为大量被劫掠文物的最终归宿,博物馆在其中扮演着重要角色。

text: play. Accusations of knowingly or unknowingly buying stolen objects have been made against several museums,
zh: [37] 多家博物馆都曾被指控明知或不知情地购买了被盗文物,

text: including the Louvre in Paris. Victoria Reid is Senior Curator at the Museum of Fine Arts in Boston.
zh: [38] 其中包括巴黎的卢浮宫。维多利亚·里德是波士顿美术博物馆的高级策展人。

text: In 2024, she helped her museum return an ancient necklace which had been taken illegally from Turkey in the 1970s.
zh: [39] 2024年,她协助自己所在的博物馆归还了一条古代项链,这条项链在20世纪70年代被非法从土耳其带走。

text: But the idea of returning artefacts to their country of origin,
zh: [40] 但把文物归还原属国这种做法,

text: something known as repatriation is controversial, as Victoria told BBC programme Business Daily.
zh: [41] 也就是所谓的“文物归还”,是有争议的,维多利亚在接受BBC《商业日报》节目采访时说道。

text: Victoria Outside of the museum world, of course, there are many opinions,
zh: [42] 维多利亚:当然,在博物馆界之外,存在着许多不同意见,

text: and I personally try to maintain a relatively middle -of -the -road approach.
zh: [43] 我个人则尽量保持一种相对中立、温和的立场。

text: I believe in museums, I believe in responsible collecting, and I believe in judicious repatriation decisions.
zh: [44] 我相信博物馆存在的价值,相信负责任的收藏方式,也相信应审慎作出文物归还的决定。

text: There are arguments both for and against repatriating artefacts, and Victoria takes a middle -of -the -road approach.
zh: [45] 关于文物归还,存在支持与反对两方面的观点,而维多利亚采取的是一种中间路线。

text: If you describe something as middle -of -the -road, you mean it's not extreme and is acceptable to most people.
zh: [46] 如果你形容某事“middle-of-the-road”(中庸的),意思是它不走极端,大多数人都能接受。

text: Whether ancient artefacts find their way back to their original homes or are displayed in museums remains an ongoing debate.
zh: [47] 古代文物究竟应该回归故土,还是继续在博物馆展出,这仍是一个持续存在的争论话题。

text: But either way, it's good to know that objects of culture and beauty are being preserved for future
zh: [48] 但无论如何,值得欣慰的是,这些兼具文化价值与美感的物品正被妥善保存,

text: generations. OK, I think it's time to reveal the answer to my question.
zh: [49] 留给后代。好了,我想是时候揭晓我那个问题的答案了。

text: I asked what the Egyptologist Howard Carter discovered in the Valley of the Kings in
zh: [50] 我问的是,埃及学家霍华德·卡特1922年在帝王谷发现的是什么,

text: 1922. Was it a the Rosetta Stone, b the Mask of Tutankhamun, or c the Ark of the Covenant?
zh: [51] 是A,罗塞塔石碑;B,图坦卡蒙的面具;还是C,约柜?

text: And I said the Mask of Tutankhamun. And you, Beth, were right. Yes!
zh: [52] 我当时说是图坦卡蒙的面具。贝丝,你答对了。没错!

text: OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learnt, starting with artefact –
zh: [53] 好,我们来回顾一下本期学到的词汇,首先是“artefact”(文物)——

text: any object made by humans which is of historical interest.
zh: [54] 指任何由人类制作、具有历史价值的物品。

text: If something is looted, it's stolen by force.
zh: [55] 如果某物“被looted”,就是说它被强行盗取了。

text: A game changer is a new element which, when introduced into a situation, completely changes it.
zh: [56] “game changer”指的是一旦引入某种情境,就会彻底改变局面的新事物。

text: A bottleneck is a problem that delays a process or stops it from progressing.
zh: [57] “bottleneck”(瓶颈)指延缓某个过程、或使其无法推进的问题。

text: To repatriate something means to return it to the country it came from.
zh: [58] “repatriate”(遣返/归还)某物,意思是把它送还到它originally所属的国家。

text: And finally, the adjective middle of the road describes things which are not extreme and are acceptable to most people.
zh: [59] 最后,形容词“middle-of-the-road”(中庸的)用来形容不走极端、大多数人都能接受的事物。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up. If you enjoyed this programme, why not check out the accompanying worksheet and quiz?
zh: [60] 我们的六分钟又到时间了。如果你喜欢这期节目,不妨去看看配套的练习题和小测验吧。

text: They're both available now at bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: [61] 它们现在都能在bbclearningenglish.com上找到。

text: See you there soon and Goodbye. Six Minute English from bbclearningenglish .com
zh: [62] 我们很快再见,再会。这里是bbclearningenglish.com的《六分钟英语》。
